heading: INSPECTION BY PROPERTY OWNER
operative_text: After the chief appraiser has submitted the appraisal records to the appraisal review board as provided by Section 25.22(a), a property owner or the owner's designated agent is entitled to inspect and copy the appraisal records relating to property of the property owner, together with supporting data, schedules, and, except as provided by Subsection (b), any other material or information held by the chief appraiser or required by Section 25.01(c) to be provided to the appraisal district under a contract for appraisal services, including material or information obtained under Section 22.27, that is obtained or used in making appraisals for the appraisal records relating to that property.
_note: Subsections (a) through (e) cover owner inspection rights, electronic/mail delivery without fee, limitations for non-residential/non-vacant land owners, private appraisal firm inspection rights, deadlines, and appraisal review board hearing restrictions pending compliance.
